page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c2.xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs, operating with a single thread per core configuration. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include any local storage or integrated GPU hardware accelerators, requiring external block storage attachment for operating system and data storage needs.

The dedicated CPU allocation ensures that the 16 physical cores deliver consistent compute performance without resource contention from other tenants. With a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the instance offers a balanced resource profile suitable for general-purpose workloads. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated core model provides predictable performance for enterprise applications, though the lack of bundled local storage and complimentary public IPv4 addresses requires careful architectural planning regarding external resource costs. This server is suited for workloads such as web serving, application servers, containerized microservices, and general-purpose business applications.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c2.xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It is configured with 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs, utilizing a single thread per core design. The instance provides 64.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not contain local storage or GPU accelerators, meaning all storage requirements must be met through network-attached block storage. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures predictable compute performance free from multi-tenant interference. This resource profile makes the instance suitable for general-purpose workloads, including web serving, application hosting, and containerized microservices.
